summary: Pi: ContinueConversation continuity survives live-session loss: a code word stated in turn 1 is recalled in turn 2 after the tmux session is killed, via native --resume off the persisted handle
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.code_word_only_spoken: never written to disk — recall proves provider-native resume

agent_review.notes: Pi: turn 1 states the code word (OK ack, never written to disk); tmux session killed to force session loss; turn 2 recalls it correctly via native --resume off the persisted handle, proving real provider-native conversation memory survives session loss, not a file-based cheat. Clean, coherent, no garbling.
